mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and consumer revolving credit indicate robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict SPX direction above 55% accuracy over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained environment directly translates to higher capacity utilization and improved profit margins, generating positive short-term equity returns as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO prints consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between INDPRO changes and SPX returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth exceeding a 1.0% quarter-over-quarter threshold signals an overheating economy that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 45% over a 5-year sample, or forward returns in expansionary quarters do not statistically differ from contractionary quarters (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and the consumer price index create stagflationary pressure, forcing aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative SPX returns with greater than 55% accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in consumer revolving credit reflects sustained household spending capacity, providing a steady tailwind for retail and service sector earnings that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, or the signal produces positive returns in less than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, elevated borrowing costs compress corporate leverage capacity and discount future cash flows, leading to negative medium-term equity returns as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5%, or the forward return differential between high-rate and low-rate periods is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.05).
